result:
Done. All three deliverables are in the working directory:

- **`script.R`** — self-contained: Okabe-Ito palette and theme declared at top, `set.seed(20260712)` before estimation, then `reshape_projoint` → `projoint(.structure = "profile_level", .estimand = "amce", .se_method = "analytical", .auto_cluster = TRUE)`, which clusters standard errors at the respondent level. I report projoint's **measurement-error-corrected** AMCEs (the headline estimand its IRR correction exists for, using the repeated flipped task).
- **`figures/amce-dotwhisker.png`** — 2250×2700 px at 300 dpi, seven attribute panels with levels ordered within attribute, reference levels plotted at zero and labeled "(ref.)", 95% intervals, no in-plot title.
- **`report.md`** — ~200-word results paragraph with the figure and caption beneath the reference.

Headline findings: high crime (−25.1 pp, 95% CI −33.4 to −16.8), a 75-minute daily drive (−23.7 pp), and housing at 40% of income (−19.8 pp) are the largest penalties; small-town (+15.8 pp) and mixed-use suburban (+14.6 pp) settings and 9/10 schools (+11.6 pp) are the largest gains. Racial composition and partisan vote share are null.

One revision cycle used: replacing deprecated `geom_errorbarh` with `geom_errorbar(orientation = "y")` and rerunning. Note for the record: projoint warned that CR2 clustered variances were non-positive-definite and fell back to Stata-style (CR1) respondent-clustered SEs — clustering is preserved, just a different small-sample correction.
